title: Power BI - SharePoint CSV Data Source Connection
description: How to connect Power BI to CSV files stored in SharePoint using the Folder connector and Power Query M

connector_choice.sharepoint_folder: Use 'SharePoint Folder' connector (Get Data > SharePoint folder) to connect to a SharePoint document library. Returns all files as rows — ideal for combining multiple CSVs.
connector_choice.sharepoint_list: Use 'SharePoint List' connector only when data lives in a SharePoint List, not a document library.
connector_choice.when_to_use_folder: Always use SharePoint Folder when CSVs are stored in a SharePoint document library. This allows combining all CSVs in a folder into one table automatically.

connection_url.format: Root site URL only — do NOT include the library path. Example: https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite
connection_url.api_version: Use [ApiVersion=15] for SharePoint Online in M code
connection_url.note: The library and folder path is filtered in M code after connection, not in the connector URL. Only modern SharePoint home page URLs are supported — classic SharePoint URLs fail to connect.
connection_url.site_picker: Power BI Desktop and Fabric Dataflow Gen2 include a SharePoint site picker that lets you browse Recent/Favourite sites instead of manually copying URLs

authentication.organizational_account: Use 'Organizational account' (OAuth2) for interactive Power BI Desktop. Sign in with a Microsoft 365 account that has Read access to the SharePoint site.
authentication.service_principal: Service principals cannot directly authenticate to SharePoint Online via Power BI datasets for scheduled refresh. Use a licensed service account (M365 user) with OAuth2 credentials configured in the dataset settings in Power BI Service.
authentication.no_gateway_needed: SharePoint Online is a cloud source. No on-premises data gateway is required for scheduled refresh.
authentication.required_permissions: The authenticating account must have at minimum Read access to the SharePoint site, the document library, and any folders leading to the CSV files.
authentication.oauth_token_expiry_note: SharePoint Online uses Microsoft Entra ID OAuth. If a scheduled refresh takes longer than 1 hour, the OAuth token may expire mid-refresh. For long-running refreshes, ensure the account credentials are refreshed or use incremental refresh to reduce refresh duration.

combine_files_wizard_pattern.description: Power BI Desktop has a built-in 'Combine Files' button when browsing a folder. It auto-generates a Transform Sample File helper query and a combining function.
auto_combine_steps: 2. Enter the site root URL (e.g. https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite), click OK
4. In Navigator, select the folder row (not an individual file)
6. In the Combine Files dialog, select the example file (defaults to First file) and configure delimiter/encoding
7. Click Transform data — Power BI auto-generates: a 'Transform Sample File' query, a 'Transform File' custom function, and the main combining query
8. The main query's leftmost column 'Source.Name' contains the original filename — useful for extracting month/year
9. In the 'Transform Sample File' query, add transformations (remove top rows, promote headers, etc.) — they apply to ALL files automatically
combine_files_wizard_pattern.auto_generated_m_structure:
The wizard produces three components in the Queries pane:
- 'Parameter1 (Sample File)': Holds a reference to the first file binary
- 'Transform Sample File': Contains the transformation steps applied to a single file
- 'Transform File': A custom function wrapping Transform Sample File, applied to every file
- Main query: Calls Table.Combine after applying the function to each file row

manual_m_vs_wizard.wizard_pros: Fast, no code required, auto-handles schema changes if all files match
manual_m_vs_wizard.wizard_cons: Generated M is verbose and harder to customize; adding filename-based date columns requires editing the auto-generated steps
manual_m_vs_wizard.manual_pros: Full control over column extraction, date parsing, and error handling
manual_m_vs_wizard.recommendation: Use the wizard for simple uniform CSVs. Use manual M when you need to extract date from filename, handle variable schemas, or add custom columns before combining.

content.special_characters_warning: Filenames containing # % $ will cause authentication errors because the filename is truncated in the SharePoint URL. Rename files to remove these characters before connecting.

scheduled_refresh_behaviour.sharepoint_online_cloud_source: No gateway required for SharePoint Online.
scheduled_refresh_behaviour.auto_sync_csv: If your dataset is created from a single CSV file on SharePoint Online/OneDrive, Power BI auto-synchronises changes about every hour. Scheduled refresh cannot be configured separately for single CSV files.
scheduled_refresh_behaviour.pbix_with_sharepoint_folder: When a .pbix uses the SharePoint Folder connector, you CAN configure scheduled refresh in Power BI Service. Go to Dataset Settings > Data source credentials > sign in with OAuth2. Then configure Schedule refresh with up to 8 refreshes/day (Pro) or 48/day (Premium).
scheduled_refresh_behaviour.new_file_pickup: When a new CSV is added to the SharePoint folder, it is automatically included in the next scheduled refresh — no Power BI changes needed. The M query dynamically re-reads all files matching the filter on every refresh.

troubleshooting.all_files_must_have_same_schema: Combining fails if files have different column names or counts. Use the Transform Sample File query to normalise before combining.
troubleshooting.filter_out_non_csv_files: If the SharePoint folder contains Excel or other files, add Extension = ".csv" filter before combining to avoid errors.
troubleshooting.error_column_not_found: When editing the Transform Sample File query, any added/removed columns can break the main Expanded step. Delete the erroring step in Applied Steps and re-expand.
troubleshooting.file_in_subfolder: SharePoint.Files returns files from all subfolders. Use Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/specific-subfolder/") to restrict to one folder.
